summary: Tested BolsaFamilia 'Todas as Escolas' consolidated view (2nd iteration). Backend: 5/7 pytest tests pass; 2 real bugs found (class_id NOT ignored in all-schools mode; school_name empty for orphan school_id). Frontend: admin sees option as 2nd item; ass_social_2 does NOT; class filter disabled, PDF disabled with tooltip, Save hidden, consolidated badge displayed, students render school_name (4/6 — matches backend gap); switching to specific school restores normal mode.

critical.issue: class_id is NOT ignored when school_id is omitted. Spec mandates 'class_id é IGNORADO em modo all-schools' but routers/bolsa_familia.py lines 806-807 applies the class_id filter unconditionally. Test: same admin query without class_id returns 6 students, with class_id=2ANO returns 1. Frontend masks this (clears selectedClass + disables Turma) but the backend contract is wrong — any direct API caller passing class_id will get partial results.

minor.issue: school_name is empty for students whose school_id has no matching row in db.schools (orphan). Example: student 62b43fd8-0040-460a-b3e7-ae0557a4112e → school_id 220d4022-ec5e-4773-8b8b-66cd9dc204ad (not in db.schools). Frontend shows nothing above the student card. Suggest fallback like 'Escola não cadastrada' OR data cleanup. 2/6 students affected in current DB.

action_items: Fix backend: ignore class_id when all_schools_mode is true. Simple change in routers/bolsa_familia.py: move `if class_id: query['class_id'] = class_id` inside the `if not all_schools_mode:` block (or guard with `and not all_schools_mode`).

Decide policy for orphan school_id in consolidated view: either backfill school_name with a sentinel ('Escola não cadastrada') so the UI label always renders, or run a data hygiene script to fix orphan school_ids on students.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/bolsa_familia.py lines 804-807: class_id filter is applied unconditionally, violating the spec for all-schools mode. The frontend mitigates by clearing selectedClass, but the backend contract should be self-protecting.
BolsaFamilia.js logic for hiding 'Todas as Escolas' uses user.role check on the client only — backend correctly enforces with 403, so this is defense-in-depth and OK.
school_name population (lines 820-830) silently falls back to '' for missing schools; consider a fallback string so the UI doesn't render an empty indigo label area inconsistently across cards.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_bf_all_schools.py (new — 7 pytest tests covering authorized/unauthorized roles, all-schools mode, per-school mode, class_id ignore, school_name population)

success_rate.backend: 71% (5/7 pytest passing — 2 real backend bugs surfaced by tests)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all assertions on dropdown ordering, role gating, disabled states, badge, and school-name labels per backend data)

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing BF students (6 across multiple schools).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes class_id-in-all-schools bug, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_bf_all_schools.py; test_admin_class_id_ignored_in_all_schools_mode should pass. For the orphan school_name issue, if main agent adopts a sentinel string, update test assertion accordingly (currently expects non-empty school_name).
rca_of_the_issue: Bug 1 (class_id): In routers/bolsa_familia.py the conditional was structured as two separate `if` blocks — school_id is guarded by all_schools_mode, but class_id is not. When admin omits school_id but passes class_id (e.g., via URL manipulation), the query becomes status+benefits+class_id, returning only students of that single class across schools. Frontend never triggers this because selectedClass is cleared when allSchoolsMode is true. Bug 2 (school_name): db.students contains school_ids that no longer exist in db.schools (deleted schools or stale FKs). school_name_map.get(school_id, '') returns '' for these, leaving the UI label empty.
